title: 10 best herbal creams for angina

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, which may contribute to its potential benefits for cardiovascular health.
Vasodilatory effects: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which could potentially help alleviate symptoms of angina by increasing oxygen delivery to the heart.
Antiplatelet properties: Ginkgo biloba may inhibit platelet aggregation, which could reduce the risk of blood clots forming in blood vessels and blocking coronary arteries.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with angina because of its unique ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help to dilate blood vessels and increase oxygen delivery to the heart. By enhancing circulation and reducing oxidative stress, Ginkgo biloba creams may help alleviate symptoms of angina, including chest pain and shortness of breath, providing relief and improving overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Helps with angina by improving blood flow to the heart muscle and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to chest pain.
Ginkgolides: Helps with angina by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound that can cause blood vessels to constrict and worsen angina symptoms.
Quercetin: Helps with angina by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, which can help to improve blood flow to the heart and alleviate chest pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are commonly used in creams due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to improve blood flow and reduce symptoms of angina.
Seeds: The seeds of the Ginkgo biloba tree are used to produce creams for angina as they contain ginkgolides, which have anti-inflammatory and vasodilating properties.

Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are also used in creams for angina due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 ginkgo biloba leaves, clean and dry them in a warm place for 2-3 days.
Infuse 1 part dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 2 parts carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil, in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discard the solids.
Mix 10% beeswax and 20% shea butter into the cooled infused oil, heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify, then whip until light and fluffy before transferring to jars.

remedy_properties: Ginkgo biloba: May help improve blood flow to the heart by dilating blood vessels, reducing the frequency and severity of anginal episodes. (Source: European Heart Journal)

Feverfew (_Tanacetum parthenium_): Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, alleviating chest pain associated with angina. (Source: Alternative Medicine Review)

Hawthorn (_Crataegus monogyna_): Has been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular diseases, including angina, by improving blood flow, lowering blood pressure, and reducing the risk of cardiac arrhythmias. (Source: Journal of Ethnopharmacology)
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tilia x europaea, also known as lime, creams helps with angina because of its potent anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating symptoms of angina. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe the cardiovascular system and promote relaxation, providing relief from chest pain and shortness of breath associated with angina. Its benefits are attributed to its ability to calm the nervous system and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Tilia x europaea creams help reduce angina symptoms by improving blood vessel function, increasing nitric oxide production, and enhancing cardiovascular health.

Terpenes (especially linalool and linalyl acetate): Terpenes in Tilia x europaea creams have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate angina by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system.

Phenolic acids (especially quercetin): Phenolic acids in Tilia x europaea creams, particularly quercetin, act as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, helping to reduce angina symptoms by protecting against cell damage and inflammation in the cardiovascular system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their vasodilatory properties, which help to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow.
Buds: The buds are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system.
Barks: The barks are used due to their anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart.
remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia x europaea flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated when they are fully open 500g.
Combine 500g of tilia x europaea flowers with 500ml of organic carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar occasionally to prevent settling.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Whip the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and 10g of cocoa butter until it reaches a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Hyperforin, a constituent of St. John's Wort, has been shown to have vasodilatory effects, which can help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks.

Hypotensive properties: St. John's Wort creams also possess hypotensive properties, meaning they can help to lower blood pressure, which is often elevated in individuals with angina, and reduce the risk of cardiac events such as heart attack or stroke.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects: The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of St. John's Wort creams may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, both of which are thought to contribute to the development of angina and other cardiovascular conditions.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with angina because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds, particularly hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing symptoms of angina. Additionally, the cream's ability to relax vascular smooth muscle cells allows for improved blood supply to the heart, alleviating chest pain and discomfort associated with angina. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative for managing angina symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic derivative that helps alleviate angina by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow and reducing ischemia.

Quercetin: A flavonoid with potent antioxidant properties that helps combat angina by scavenging free radicals, inhibiting platelet aggregation, and improving endothelial function to enhance blood vessel relaxation.

N-feruloylserotonin: An alkaloid that contributes to the anxiolytic and anti-ischemic effects of Hypericum perforatum by modulating serotonin levels, reducing stress and anxiety, and improving cardiovascular function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate angina symptoms.
Flowers: They are used for their naphthoquinone compounds, specifically hyperforin, which helps reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation to alleviate angina pain.

Roots: They are used due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation to alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers in a 500 milliliter glass jar with a lid.
Combine 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as jojoba oil with 200 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Steep the herb extract in the oil and wax mixture for 2 hours at 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then add 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil.
Pour the final mixture into a 500 milliliter tin container and let it set for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Corn Silk has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina.

Cardiovascular protective effects: The flavonoids present in Corn Silk may help protect against cardiovascular disease by improving blood vessel function, reducing blood pressure, and preventing platelet aggregation, all of which can help alleviate angina symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Corn Silk's antioxidant properties may help neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to angina.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ruscus aculeatus, also known as dog holly, creams helps with angina because it is believed to have vasodilatory properties, which can help to improve blood flow to the heart. This increased blood flow can reduce the frequency and severity of angina attacks. Additionally, Ruscus aculeatus may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often contributing factors to angina. By addressing these underlying issues, Ruscus aculeatus creams may provide relief from angina symptoms and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Ruscus aculeatus creams have antioxidant and vasodilatory properties, which can help reduce blood pressure, improve blood flow to the heart, and alleviate symptoms of angina.

Saponins: Saponins in Ruscus aculeatus creams have anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular protective effects, which can help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, improve blood lipid profiles, and prevent platelet aggregation, ultimately reducing the risk of angina.

Terpenoids: Terpenoids in Ruscus aculeatus creams possess vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relax blood vessels, reduce blood pressure, and improve blood flow to the heart, thereby alleviating symptoms of angina.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Used for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of angina.
Leaves: Used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh ruscus aculeatus leaves and dry them in a warm place for 24 hours.
Infuse the dried leaves in 100ml of sweet almond oil at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Combine 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler and melt them slowly.
Mix the melted beeswax and coconut oil with the infused oil and pour the mixture into small jars to set.

remedy_properties: Cardioprotective flavonoids: Hawthorn's flavonoid content helps to protect the heart from damage by reducing inflammation, improving blood vessel function, and enhancing the body's natural defenses against cardiovascular disease.
Vasodilatory saponins: Hawthorn's saponin compounds help to dilate blood vessels, increasing blood flow to the heart and reducing angina symptoms such as chest pain and shortness of breath.

Anti-inflammatory triterpenoids: Hawthorn's triterpenoid compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to improved cardiovascular health and reduced angina symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, creams helps with angina because it is rich in flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which have been shown to improve cardiovascular health by enhancing blood flow, reducing blood pressure, and preventing platelet aggregation. This can help alleviate the symptoms of angina by providing the heart with more oxygen and nutrients. Additionally, hawthorn's vasodilatory properties can help relax blood vessels, further improving blood flow and reducing the severity of angina attacks.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help alleviate angina symptoms by improving blood vessel function, reducing inflammation, and preventing platelet aggregation.

Triterpenes: Specifically, ursolic acid and oleanolic acid are triterpenes found in Crataegus monogyna that help alleviate angina by improving myocardial contractility, reducing oxidative stress, and modulating calcium channels.

Flavonol glycosides (e.g., quercetin-3-O-rutinoside): These compounds help alleviate angina symptoms by reducing inflammation, improving endothelial function, and preventing platelet aggregation, ultimately contributing to improved blood flow to the heart muscle.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to improve cardiovascular health.
Fruits: The fruits, or hawthorn berries, contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which may help to reduce angina symptoms by improving blood flow and lowering blood pressure.
Barks: Hawthorn bark is also a source of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help to improve cardiovascular function and reduce angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh hawthorn berries at the end of summer or beginning of autumn.
Dry the berries in a well-ventilated area at 30°c for 24 hours.
Grind 200g of dried hawthorn berries into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 200g of shea butter and 50g of hawthorn powder in a double boiler.
Whip 200g of coconut oil with 100g of hawthorn cream base for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Herbal horse chestnut creams contain compounds that help to dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing symptoms of angina. This helps to reduce chest pain and discomfort associated with reduced blood flow.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal horse chestnut creams help to neutralize free radicals that can damage heart tissue and contribute to cardiovascular disease, potentially reducing the risk of angina and other cardiovascular conditions.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Horse chestnut extract has been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help to alleviate symptoms of angina such as chest pain and discomfort, while also reducing the risk of chronic diseases like heart failure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aesculus hippocastanum, also known as horse chestnut, creams helps with angina because it contains a compound called aescin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart. This increased blood flow helps to reduce the frequency and severity of angina attacks by providing the heart with more oxygen. Additionally, aescin has been shown to have a protective effect on the endothelium, the inner lining of blood vessels, which can become damaged in people with angina.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Aesculus hippocastanum help alleviate angina by reducing blood pressure and improving blood circulation, thereby increasing oxygen delivery to the heart.

Isoflavones (e.g., aesculetin and aesculin): Isoflavones in Aesculus hippocastanum may help reduce angina symptoms by possessing antioxidant properties, which protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress and inflammation.

Sterols (e.g., beta-sitosterol): Sterols in Aesculus hippocastanum may help alleviate angina symptoms by inhibiting platelet aggregation and improving lipid profiles, thus reducing the risk of cardiovascular events.
remedy_parts: Barks: Aesculus hippocastanum barks are used due to their potential vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Aesculus hippocastanum seeds are used due to their potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health.
Leaves: Aesculus hippocastanum leaves are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh aesculus hippocastanum leaves and stems from a well-managed forest area at dawn.
Dry the harvested plant material in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 3 days.
Combine 50 grams of dried aesculus hippocastanum with 200 milliliters of jojoba oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally with a wooden spoon.
Filter the infused mixture through a coffee filter into a clean glass container and store at room temperature.

remedy_properties: Cardiotonic properties: Herbal grape creams contain cardiotonic compounds that help to strengthen the heart's contractions, improving blood circulation and reducing symptoms of angina such as chest pain and shortness of breath.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in herbal grape creams help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the cardiovascular system, thereby reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can contribute to angina.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal grape creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can alleviate symptoms of angina such as chest pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera, also known as grape, creams helps with angina because they contain flavonoids and resveratrol, which are potent antioxidants that help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. This, in turn, helps to alleviate the symptoms of angina by increasing oxygen supply to the heart muscle and reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks. Additionally, Vitis vinifera creams may help to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: A flavonoid found in Vitis vinifera, quercetin helps with angina by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to chest pain.

Resveratrol: A stilbenoid polyphenol, resveratrol has been shown to help with angina by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the coronary arteries, which can reduce chest pain and improve heart function.

Ferulic acid: A phenolic acid, ferulic acid helps with angina by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to chest pain and cardiovascular disease.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate angina symptoms.
Seeds: They are rich in flavonoids, particularly vitisins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that may help reduce angina symptoms.
Fruits: They are a source of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that may help alleviate angina symptoms due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried vitis vinifera in 2 cups of carrier oil for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infusion using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of beeswax with 1/2 cup of distilled water in a small saucepan over low heat.
Combine 2 tablespoons of the infused vitis vinifera oil with 2 tablespoons of the beeswax mixture stirring constantly.
Pour the cream mixture into a clean container and allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to anginal pain.

Calming and relaxing effect: Chamomile's soothing properties can help calm anxiety and stress, which are common triggers for angina attacks, and promote relaxation, reducing symptoms like chest tightness and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Chamomile's antioxidant compounds can help protect the heart cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to cardiovascular disease and worsen anginal symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with angina because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The chamazulene and bisabolol compounds found in chamomile have been shown to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks. Additionally, chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system can help alleviate stress and anxiety, common triggers for angina. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, chamomile creams can provide relief from angina symptoms and promote overall cardiovascular well-being.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a calcium channel blocker, helping to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, thus reducing angina symptoms.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in blood vessels, improve blood flow, and alleviate angina pain.
α-Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the heart from damage, improve blood flow, and reduce inflammation that can contribute to angina.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Matricaria chamomilla are commonly used due to their high content of apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and calming properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with angina.
Seeds: The seeds are used due to their high content of matricaria ester, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation related to angina.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a reputable source to ensure quality.
Combine 30 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500 milliliters of coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Whip 250 grams of unsalted shea butter with 20 milliliters of the infused oil until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Cardiac stimulant and vasodilator properties: Valerian helps to increase blood flow to the heart, reducing symptoms of angina by improving oxygen delivery to the heart muscle.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to angina attacks. Its anti-inflammatory effects also help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation.

Muscle relaxant and sedative properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing properties can help reduce the frequency and severity of anginal episodes by calming spasms and contractions in the coronary arteries, while its sedative effects may help alleviate anxiety and stress that can exacerbate angina symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with angina because they contain valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which have been shown to have a positive effect on heart health. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce blood pressure and improve circulation, alleviating angina symptoms. Additionally, valerian's natural sedative properties can help to calm the heart and promote relaxation, further contributing to a reduction in angina episodes. remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which helps to reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to angina symptoms.
Valeranone: Exhibits vasodilatory properties, helping to improve blood flow to the heart muscle and reduce the workload on the heart, thereby alleviating angina symptoms.

Bornyl acetate: Displays antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to angina development.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used due to their high content of valerenic acid, which is believed to have a sedative effect and help alleviate angina symptoms.
Leaves: Utilized for their valerenic acid content and potential vasodilatory properties, which can help improve blood flow and reduce angina episodes.
Barks: Employed for their valerenic acid content and potential ability to relax blood vessels, thereby reducing strain on the heart and alleviating angina symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full maturity.
Dry the valeriana officinalis roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 2 to 3 weeks.
Grind 10 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 5 grams of the valeriana officinalis powder with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Store the herbal valeriana officinalis cream in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help protect the heart from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to angina symptoms.
Vasodilatory effects: The herb's vasodilating properties help widen blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the coronary arteries, which can contribute to angina symptoms and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with angina because of its ability to improve cardiovascular health. The herb contains flavonoids and terpenes, which have been shown to dilate blood vessels, reduce inflammation, and lower blood pressure. These actions can help increase blood flow to the heart, reducing symptoms of angina such as chest pain and shortness of breath. remedy_constituents: Rosmarin acid (Rosmarinic acid): This phenolic compound has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thus alleviating angina symptoms.

Volatile oil (Mainly consisting of citral and linalool): The volatile oil in Melissa officinalis has vasodilatory effects, which can help relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce blood pressure, ultimately easing angina symptoms.

Apigenin: This flavonoid has a protective effect on the cardiovascular system by reducing inflammation, improving blood flow, and preventing platelet aggregation, all of which can help alleviate angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and essential oils, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for angina treatment.
Flowers: They are used for their calming and antispasmodic effects, which help reduce anxiety and alleviate symptoms associated with angina.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm area with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Infuse 200 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves in 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear herbal extract.
Mix the herbal extract with beeswax and shea butter to create a smooth and moisturizing cream base.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with angina because they contain eucalyptol, a natural compound that dilates blood vessels and improves blood flow to the heart. This increased circulation reduces the strain on the heart muscle, alleviating symptoms of angina such as chest pain and shortness of breath. Cineole: A natural anti-inflammatory agent, cineole may help to reduce inflammation in the coronary arteries, which can contribute to anginal episodes.
Gaultherin: A compound with antioxidant properties, gaultherin may help to protect against oxidative stress and damage to the heart muscle cells, which can worsen angina symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eucalyptus globulus leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves in a warm, dry place for 24 hours.
Steep one tablespoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in two cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Mix the cooled infusion with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil at 160°f for 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a sterilized container, label and store in a cool, dark place for up to six months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh artemisia absinthium leaves and stems, ensuring at least 4 ounces for this recipe preparation.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or low heat methods for 24 hours.
Grind dried artemisia absinthium into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, resulting in approximately 2 tablespoons.
Combine ground artemisia absinthium with 1/4 cup of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan over low heat, stirring for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool before transferring it to an amber glass container, allowing it to mature for at least 2 weeks before use.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects, which can help to improve blood flow to the heart and reduce anginal symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves from local organic farmers.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 3 minutes.
Combine the ground herb powder with 200ml of coconut oil in a heatproof glass measuring cup.
Place the mixture in a double boiler over low heat and stir occasionally for 30 minutes until melted.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and store at room temperature.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of volatile oils, which help to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids that have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce oxidative stress in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and flowers, rinsing them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine 20g of dried verbena officinalis herb with 100ml of olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for at least 14 days, shaking daily to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding solids.
Mix the infused oil with 20g of beeswax and heat gently over low heat until melted, then pour into sterilized tubes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with angina because they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate chest pain and discomfort associated with coronary artery disease. The extracts of fennel, such as anethole, have been shown to improve blood flow to the heart muscle, reducing the frequency and severity of anginal attacks. Additionally, fennel's natural vasodilatory effects can help to relax blood vessels, further decreasing cardiac workload and promoting overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Fennel cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to angina symptoms.
Vasodilatory effects: The vasodilatory compounds present in fennel cream, such as anethole, help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, reducing anginal episodes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina symptoms, and also alleviate pain and discomfort associated with angina.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots contain a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow, making them effective in treating angina.
Barks: The bark of the fennel plant contains alkaloids that have anti-spasmodic properties, helping to relax constricted blood vessels and alleviate symptoms of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and chop them into small pieces to ensure optimal potency.
Combine one part chopped foeniculum vulgare leaves with two parts cold-pressed olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily, allowing the herbal extracts to infuse.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix the resulting herbal extract with 10% beeswax and 5% coconut oil, stirring until smooth and creamy, then pour into sterile containers for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese thoroughwort creams helps with angina because they contain a unique combination of herbs that help to dilate blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce inflammation. The cream's active ingredients, such as Danshen and Sanchi, have been shown to relax the muscles in the blood vessel walls, allowing for increased blood flow to the heart muscle. This can help to alleviate symptoms of angina, including chest pain and shortness of breath, by providing the heart with a more stable supply of oxygen-rich blood.
remedy_properties: Sodium Ferulate: This compound has anti-thrombotic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help prevent platelet aggregation and reduce inflammation in blood vessels, alleviating symptoms of angina.
Corydaline: This alkaloid has a direct effect on the cardiovascular system, dilating blood vessels and improving blood circulation, which can help relieve chest pain and alleviate symptoms of angina.

Berberine: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of angina attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making it effective in relieving angina symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Contain antiplatelet properties that prevent blood clots from forming, reducing the risk of cardiac arrest associated with angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh bupleurum falcatum roots weighing 30 grams and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Dry the roots in a warm dark place for at least 7 days or until they become brittle and break easily.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using an electric grinder and sift it through a 100-mesh screen to ensure uniform size particles.
Combine the powdered bupleurum falcatum with 250 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating the mixture gently over low heat for 1 hour and stirring occasionally.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool completely before transferring it to sterile glass jars with tight-fitting lids for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with angina because of its unique combination of calming and relaxing properties. The herb's ability to soothe the nervous system reduces stress and anxiety, common triggers for anginal episodes. Additionally, lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds help alleviate chest pain and discomfort associated with coronary artery disease. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, English lavender creams provide a natural solution to help manage angina symptoms and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Lavandula angustifolia oil's vasodilatory effect: This property helps to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce symptoms of angina by increasing blood flow to the heart.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Lavender cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate that may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina symptoms.

Anxiolytic and stress-reducing properties: Lavender's calming effects can help alleviate anxiety and stress, both of which are common triggers for angina episodes. By reducing stress levels, lavender cream may help prevent or reduce frequency of angina attacks.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, the roots of English lavender help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, making them suitable for use in medicinal creams for angina.

Flowers: The flowers of English lavender contain a high concentration of luteolin and apigenin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing symptoms of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, well-ventilated area for 7 days or until crumbly and brittle.
Combine the dried flowers with 50 grams of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler, heating gently over medium heat.
Steep the mixture for 20 minutes, allowing the lavandula angustifolia extract to infuse into the coconut oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, discarding solids and transferring infused oil to glass containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with angina because they contain potent antioxidants, such as flavonoids and vitamins A, C, and E, which help to improve blood flow to the heart. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the cardiovascular system, alleviating chest pain and discomfort associated with angina. Additionally, dandelion's diuretic effects help to lower blood pressure and alleviate fluid retention, further contributing to its beneficial effects on cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Dandelion cream contains cardiac glycosides, which can help regulate heart rhythm and reduce symptoms of angina by improving blood flow to the heart.

Inulin: Inulin, a type of fiber found in dandelion roots, has been shown to help lower cholesterol levels and improve blood lipid profiles, reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and alleviating anginal symptoms.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in dandelion cream, such as sesquiterpenes, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the cardiovascular system, contributing to a reduction in angina symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating angina symptoms.
Buds: Containing salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps to thin the blood and prevent platelet aggregation, reducing the risk of cardiovascular events.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 20 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Combine weighed ingredients with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in double boiler.
Heat mixture under low heat for 30 minutes, then remove from heat and steep for 4 hours.
Strain infused oil through cheesecloth and discard solids, retaining 50 milliliters of infused liquid.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 40 milliliters of petroleum jelly, adding infused liquid and stirring until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian creams helps with angina because they contain the herb Gentiana lutea, which has been traditionally used to improve circulation and reduce inflammation. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help to relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and improving oxygen flow to the heart. Additionally, yellow gentian's vasodilatory effects can help to dilate coronary arteries, increasing blood flow to areas of the heart affected by angina. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate symptoms of angina, providing relief from chest pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: Gentiana lutea contains cardenolides, which can help regulate heart rhythm and increase cardiac contractility, potentially reducing symptoms of angina.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The plant's flavonoids and terpenoids have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve blood flow to the heart, alleviating anginal pain.

Vasodilatory effects: Gentiana lutea has been shown to cause vasodilation (widening of blood vessels), which can help reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of angina by increasing blood flow to the heart.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yellow gentian plant contain flavonoids and alkaloids that help to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, making them effective in reducing symptoms of angina.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the yellow gentian plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried gentiana lutea roots in sufficient quantity for the desired quantity of cream.
Grind 50 grams of dried gentiana lutea roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20 grams of ground gentiana lutea with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a glass jar.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and let it cool to room temperature before storing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Soapwort creams helps with angina because they contain saponin, a natural foaming agent that can help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation. This increase in blood flow helps to reduce the frequency and severity of angina episodes by allowing more oxygen-rich blood to reach the heart muscle. Additionally, soapwort's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce inflammation and scar tissue formation in the coronary arteries, further contributing to its beneficial effects on angina symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Soapwort's saponin content has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a common underlying factor in many cardiovascular conditions, including angina.
Antioxidant activity: The plant's antioxidants may help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of heart disease.
Cardiotonic properties: Soapwort has been traditionally used to support cardiac function and improve circulation, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness for this purpose.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce blood pressure and improve circulation, alleviating symptoms of angina.
Barks: Containing flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and discomfort associated with angina.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh saponaria officinalis leaves measure 20 grams of leaves and clean them thoroughly.
Infuse the leaves in 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil for six hours steeping time.
Strain the infusion using cheesecloth or a coffee filter squeeze out excess oil without pressing.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 30 milliliters of coconut oil and 20 milliliters of infused oil heat gently stirring occasionally.
Pour the mixture into clean glass jars filling to the top let cool at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for angina are topical preparations containing plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms of angina, a condition characterized by chest pain due to reduced blood flow to the heart. The best herbal creams for angina include Ginkgo biloba, Crataegus monogyna, Panax ginseng, Ruscus aculeatus, Zingiber officinale, Centella asiatica, Lavandula angustifolia, Rauvolfia serpentina, Paeonia lactiflora, and Withania somnifera. If left untreated, angina can lead to shortness of breath, fatigue, and decreased mobility, severely impacting daily activities and overall quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to angina that are treated with herbal creams are peripheral artery disease, Raynaud's phenomenon, and intermittent claudication. Herbal creams containing ingredients like ginkgo biloba, grape seed extract, and capsaicin may help improve blood flow, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms such as pain, numbness, and tingling in the affected areas. These creams can be used topically to provide relief from discomfort and promote overall circulation health.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with angina is a blend of hawthorn, ginkgo biloba, and ashwagandha. Hawthrorn, rich in flavonoids, improves blood flow and lowers blood pressure. Ginkgo biloba enhances circulation and reduces inflammation. Ashwagandha, an adaptogen, reduces stress and promotes cardiovascular health. These creams can be applied topically to the chest area, promoting relaxation and reducing symptoms of angina. It is essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have pre-existing medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba creams helps with angina because they improve blood flow to the heart, reducing the frequency and severity of chest pain episodes. The antioxidants and flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba help to relax and dilate blood vessels, increasing oxygen delivery to the heart muscle. This natural vasodilation effect reduces the strain on the heart and alleviates symptoms of angina, allowing individuals to engage in physical activities without discomfort. Regular use of Ginkgo biloba creams may also enhance overall cardiovascular health.

Crataegus monogyna creams helps with angina because they are rich in flavonoids and polyphenols, which have been shown to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation. The herb, also known as hawthorn, has been traditionally used to support cardiovascular health by dilating blood vessels and increasing the heart's pumping efficiency. This can lead to a reduction in symptoms such as chest pain and shortness of breath, making it a potential natural remedy for managing angina. Regular use may also help to improve overall heart health.

Panax ginseng creams helps with angina because they contain bioactive compounds that improve cardiovascular health. The creams stimulate blood flow and increase nitric oxide production, which relaxes blood vessels and lowers blood pressure. This reduction in blood pressure and improved circulation allows the heart to receive more oxygen, alleviating symptoms of angina. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of Panax ginseng creams may help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to improved cardiovascular health and reduced angina symptoms.

Ruscus aculeatus creams helps with angina because they are rich in flavonoids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to relax blood vessels, improve circulation, and reduce oxidative stress, all of which are beneficial in the treatment of angina. By reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular function, Ruscus aculeatus creams may help to alleviate symptoms of angina, such as chest pain and shortness of breath, providing relief and improving overall cardiovascular health. Regular use may also help to prevent future angina episodes.

Zingiber officinale creams helps with angina because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory properties. The cream's topical application can help to relax blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing the workload on the heart muscle. This can help to alleviate the symptoms of angina, such as chest pain and shortness of breath. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger in the cream can also help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, further contributing to its beneficial effects on angina.

Centella asiatica creams helps with angina because they contain compounds that improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system. The herb's active ingredients, such as asiaticoside and madecassoside, have been shown to enhance nitric oxide production, which widens blood vessels and increases oxygen delivery to the heart. By mitigating oxidative stress and promoting vascular health, Centella asiatica creams may help alleviate angina symptoms and support overall cardiovascular well-being. Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with angina because they contain high levels of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have a positive impact on cardiovascular health. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the frequency and severity of angina attacks. Additionally, the calming effects of lavender oil may help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate angina symptoms, promoting overall heart health.

Rauvolfia serpentina creams helps with angina because they contain active compounds like reserpine, serpentinine, and rauhutin, which have a positive effect on the cardiovascular system. These compounds work by dilating blood vessels, thereby improving blood flow to the heart muscles. This increased blood flow helps to reduce the frequency and severity of angina attacks, providing relief from the discomfort and pain associated with reduced blood flow to the heart. The creams can help to relax blood vessels, improving blood flow to the heart and reducing the workload on the heart. Additionally, Paeonia lactiflora's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to angina. The herb helps to improve cardiovascular health by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to angina. Additionally, Withania somnifera may help to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure, alleviating symptoms of angina. The cream can be applied topically to the affected area, providing localized relief and promoting overall cardiovascular well-being.
